title: Refactor a command parser into binary pattern-matched dispatch
prompt:
Given:

```
def handle(cmd_bin) do
  if String.starts_with?(cmd_bin, "AUTH") do
    payload = String.slice(cmd_bin, 5, 1024)
    auth(payload)
  else
    if String.starts_with?(cmd_bin, "LIST ") do
      payload = String.slice(cmd_bin, 5, 1024)
      list(payload)
    else
      if String.starts_with?(cmd_bin, "QUIT") do
        quit()
      else
        {:error, :unknown}
      end
    end
  end
end
```

Rewrite as multi-clause function heads using binary pattern matching in each head. Include `def handle(<<"AUTH ", payload::binary>>)`, `def handle(<<"LIST ", payload::binary>>)`, `def handle("QUIT")`, and a catch-all. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.CommandRouter do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Nested if-chain → binary-matched heads. Synthesis of nested-if cleanup + binary pattern matching.
